title: Reverse-Engineer Synthetic Strategy Thresholds
summary: The competition appears inspired by a generated F1 strategy dataset whose public description emphasizes adapted distributions and leakage prevention.
rationale: The competition appears inspired by a generated F1 strategy dataset whose public description emphasizes adapted distributions and leakage prevention. If labels come from a simulator or heuristic, hidden breakpoints in TyreLife, RaceProgress, Stint, and degradation may matter more than hand-set tyre-life constants.
implementation_hint: Fit a shallow EBM/GAM/RuleFit or highly binned HistGradientBoosting model on core numeric features plus pairwise interactions. Use PDP/SHAP to find sharp cutpoints, add those threshold flags to the main GBDT/AutoGluon stack, and blend the rule model raw scores.
expected_effect: Can recover target-generation thresholds missed by smooth ratio features; likely small but direct AUC lift near the plateau.
risk: May overfit random CV unless checked with Race_Year/Driver grouped validation.
